title: Hello world image only available for amd64 ?
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Looks like the new binary "Hello, World!" is not available for the arm64 architecture ?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run quay.io/podman/hello`

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: quay.io/podman/hello:latest: image not known`

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.6
Built:        Wed Dec  8 19:41:11 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:
Version:      3.2.1
API Version:  3.2.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Mac M1

comments.body:
cc @TomSweeneyRedHat 

* #13079

The shell version might have been more portable (but still needed two images, anyway)

```
INFO[0022] READY. Run `limactl shell podman` to open the shell. 
INFO[0022] Message from the instance "podman":          
To run `podman` on the host (assumes podman-remote is installed), run the following commands:
------
podman system connection add lima "unix:///Users/m1/.lima/podman/sock/podman.sock"
podman system connection default lima
podman run quay.io/podman/hello
------
```

Rebuilding the image works fine, so it's more an issue of making it multi-arch (amd64 and arm64) on quay.io.

https://github.com/containers/podman/tarball/main/contrib/helloimage

Unfortunately it is **not** possible to build it with `podman machine` since it lacks the necessary qemu binaries.

* https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12144

-----

`podman build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -t podman/hello .`

```
exec container process `/bin/sh`: Exec format error`
```

To fix it: `podman machine ssh sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static`

```console
% uname
Darwin
% arch 
arm64
% podman run -it docker.io/amd64/alpine arch  
x86_64
% podman run -it docker.io/arm64v8/alpine arch
aarch64
```
Probably should build for IBM OSs also.
I could, but do we have any way to test some of them?  I'd looked into this last night, but couldn't figure out how I'd validate that the arm64 say worked....

You can use Lima to start a Podman VM in emulation (or you could use an aarch64 cloud instance)

Theoretically it should support running ppc64le and s390x too, but you need to add images

EDIT: it would need a new feature, as well:

`FATA[0012] the YAML is invalid, saved the buffer as "lima.REJECTED.yaml": field `arch` must be "x86_64" or "aarch64" , got "ppc64le" `

Adding `qemu-user-static` allows you to run arm64 binaries on your amd64 host, as well.
Using the `--platform` flag.

You can only have one image of the same name, though. So might have to untag the regular one, it is an annoying shortcoming (due to arch being an afterthought).

This should go through the same build process as the other podman images such as `quay.io/podman/stable`
cc @cevich

I'll take this on.  I'm currently working on a replacement for the github-actions workflow that builds our multi-arch images and manifest list.  I will include building this one in addition, it seems to come from `contrib/helloimage/Containerfile`.

Ref: https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/97

@cevich thanks, and good thought @Luap99 .  Chris, @afbjorklund thought there might be problems with the c code across the different architectures.  See prior comments.  If that is the case, I have a bash script and containerfile to build the image tucked away.  If you'd like, I could send them to you or add them to the repo.  

The bash should work anywhere, but the resulting image isn't as tiny as the C implementation.

okay, thanks for the heads up.  I'll keep an eye out for this.  I've got a POC of my new tooling (awaiting review) and was successful getting it to build the unstable podman image.  Though it takes nearly an hour and a half :cry: 

Ref: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13478

> See prior comments

What comments ?

But I think a shell script (`/bin/sh`) is fine as well, would avoid using bash
Note, the underlying automation work for this is complete, I'm just waiting on the job to actually run to confirm this is fixed and can be closed.

Okay, looks like [the new build automation](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4557825379663872?logs=main#L202-L230) (log) successfully pushed `quay.io/podman/hello:latest`.  It seems to work for me locally, and there were no errors compiling it under foreign architectures.   Please re-open this and/or let me know if there are any problems with the image.

Thanks, looks ok now.

`podman run --platform=linux/amd64 quay.io/podman/hello`

`podman run --platform=linux/arm64 quay.io/podman/hello`
